session_summary: Implemented full parry system with Fighter/Mage/Rogue variants, created data assets, wired to Player prefab
done: ParryData ScriptableObject: all timing/damage/effect values designer-editable
ParrySystem MonoBehaviour: core parry logic with class-specific behavior
CombatEnums: added Parrying state to CombatState
EnemyAttackHitbox: parry check before damage + ApplyParryEffects helper (stun + counter-damage)
EnemyProjectile: parry check + ReflectProjectile for Mage spell mirror
PlayerControllerScript: Parry input callback, cached ParrySystem ref, RefreshAbilities updated
EnemyController: added ApplyStun(float duration) public method
InputSystem_Actions: added Parry action (F key / gamepad left trigger)
Created 3 ParryData assets: WarriorParry, MageParry, RogueParry
Wired ParryData assets to Warrior, Mage, Rogue JobClassData assets
Added ParrySystem component to Player prefab
Wired Parry input action event in PlayerInput on Player prefab
decisions: Parry is a core combat mechanic, not a skill-tree skill — separate component + data asset
Single ParrySystem component handles all 3 variants via ParryType enum from data
Damage interception at EnemyAttackHitbox/EnemyProjectile OnTriggerEnter2D before ApplyDamage
Reflected projectiles switch to PlayerAttack layer (existing layer) to hit enemies
Shadow step repositions behind attacker using attacker facing direction
next_steps: Playtest each class parry in-editor: Fighter stun+counter, Mage reflect, Rogue shadow step
Add parry animations per class (currently no animation triggers)
Assign parrySound and parryVFXPrefab to the 3 ParryData assets when audio/VFX are ready
Consider adding parry to Beginner class (currently null parryData = no parry)
